sections.vendors: Asda has confirmed proposals to outsource George.com delivery to DHL from January 2027, moving online-clothing fulfilment from its own depots in Staffordshire, Northamptonshire and Tyne & Wear to DHL's Derby site — around 1,200 staff affected. No changes reported to garment vendors themselves; the PDS 'Sourcing as a Service' arrangement (Bangladesh, India, Turkey) remains the EMEA backbone.
sections.strategy: Asda is cutting around 150 head office jobs to fund the turnaround plan, with Allan Leighton insisting the recovery is 'working' despite a sales slowdown — George remains the standout, having outperformed its market in FY25 and now ranking as the UK's second-largest fashion brand by volume. Menswear is the stated growth priority, with added investment as George targets share from high-street rivals. The 10-store standalone George concept rollout continues, with Peterborough named as the third site after Leeds and Hull's double-digit growth.
sections.pricing: Peak back-to-school trading is underway: George is running 20% off school uniform online and in-store, backed by its 100-day satisfaction guarantee, with summer menswear clearance from £1. Competitive backdrop is intensifying — Tesco's F&F relaunched online in May with 2,200+ products, while George (6th) still comfortably outranks F&F (18th) in YouGov's UK fashion brand popularity table.
sections.launches: The premium 'Studio Edit' menswear collection launched in June continues to headline — updated fits, textured fabrics and refined palettes at accessible price points — alongside digital investment in outfitting tools and editorial imagery to lift conversion and basket size. The 2026 school uniform range is now the promotional focus for July.
sections.gossip: Ex-Primark CEO Paul Marchant's advisory role on George's long-term strategy (in place since May) remains live and continues to be read as a signal Asda wants George to run the Primark playbook on value and scale. Trade press framing has shifted from Asda being 3rd in UK clothing by volume to openly targeting Primark's #1 spot; George is already #1 by volume in kidswear.
soWhat: Back-to-school is George's biggest volume event and it's discounting 20% — expect aggressive cost pressure on uniform/basics programmes and early positioning for BTS 2027 commitments.
Menswear is the funded growth category (Studio Edit, above-market growth) — suppliers with menswear capability in textured fabrics and elevated basics have a live opening to pitch.

Cost discipline is tightening across the business (150 head office cuts, George.com fulfilment outsourced to DHL from Jan 2027) — anticipate continued margin and lead-time pressure, and confirm logistics touchpoints before the 2027 fulfilment switch.
